assumptions: The developer has not supplied a Workday customer organization, Developer Site account, Personal or Enterprise Extend account, development tenant, production tenant, subscription, or administrator role.
No REST, SOAP, Graph, Extend, or Integration app surface has been preselected.
Public documentation is the only available evidence; no customer tenant credentials are assumed.

developer_goal: Obtain required Workday developer and tenant access, choose an API or app surface, and reach that surface's earliest official authenticated first-success result.

first_success.outcome: For a selected surface such as Extend App Builder: show a Development tenant connection with a green status indicator, or for a selected REST path: complete the first authenticated business-object response defined by that guide.
first_success.observable_signal: Depends on the unresolved surface: App Builder green connection indicator is one documented candidate terminal; REST Explorer and Integration apps define different terminals.

route_selection.surface: Workday Developer Site and tenant-backed API or Extend surfaces
route_selection.rule: Official entry points present REST, SOAP, Graph, Extend, and Integration apps as peer routes. Prefer a path without unavailable entitlement only after confirming which program and tenant access the developer actually has.

candidates.name: REST API Explorer authenticated business-object call
candidates.reason: Requires Developer Site plus tenant security configuration that public docs do not grant from account creation alone.

candidates.name: Extend App Builder connected to a Development tenant
candidates.reason: Company administrator, Extend subscription, and development-tenant provisioning gates differ from direct REST.

candidates.name: Integration apps / Orchestrate getting started
candidates.reason: Peer official route with different tools and entitlements.

candidates.name: SOAP or Graph API first call
candidates.reason: Alternate protocols with distinct security and gateway setup.

required_path.action: Obtain Workday Developer Site access for the intended company or program.

required_fields.label: Developer Site access path
required_fields.notes: Public docs describe administrator invitation and entitlements rather than a fully self-serve cold signup inventory.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can sign in to the Developer Site Console.

required_path.action: Choose REST, SOAP, Graph, Extend, or Integration apps as the development surface.
required_path.observable_result: One official getting-started guide becomes the candidate route.

required_path.action: Obtain the tenant, security configuration, and administrator grants required by the selected surface.
required_path.observable_result: A development or eligible tenant is available to the developer identity.

required_path.action: Follow the selected guide's register-client, authenticate, build, or connect steps through its documented terminal result.
required_path.observable_result: The selected guide's first authenticated business-object or app-connection success is visible.

prerequisites.description: Developer Site and Workday tenant access are required for owned authenticated data or app paths.
Company Administrators create and manage Workday Cloud Platform Development tenants under Extend contract limits.

external_gates.description: Company administrator invitation, customer entitlement, subscription agreement, or innovation-services opt-in may be required before Developer Site use.
REST API security depends on Workday domain and authentication configuration in a tenant the developer does not create alone from public signup.
API client registration and grant-type choices are constrained by tenant and Developer Site permissions.

unavoidable_waits.description: Development tenant build and administrator email notification must finish before tenant sign-in.
Administrator invitation and entitlement provisioning may precede any developer console work.

platform_outcomes.description: Workday can provision Development tenants requested by Company Administrators and email sign-in details when ready.
Authenticated REST or Extend flows return tenant-backed API or app-connection results after security setup.

excluded.item: Reading the full Workday API catalog without selecting a surface
excluded.reason: Discovery reading is not a measured product action.
Official development guidance centers on Development or non-production tenants.
Workday documents it as vulnerable and recommends PKCE authorization-code flows instead.

sources.title: Workday APIs overview
evidence_supported: Developer Site API entry and platform surface overview

sources.title: Authenticate Using the Client Credentials Grant Type
Manage Users in Companies
evidence_supported: Administrator invitation and company user management

sources.title: Getting Started with Integration Apps
evidence_supported: Integration apps as a peer starting surface

sources.title: Call Workday SOAP APIs Using the API Gateway
evidence_supported: SOAP as an alternate protocol path

uncertainties.question: Which Workday platform surface and business operation should define first success: REST, SOAP, Graph, Extend, or Integration apps?
uncertainties.impact: Protocol, product, tenant, actors, security, tools, and terminal result all change, so lexicographic selection cannot pick among peer official entry points.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded surface and operation choice, then a complete cold path against that guide.

uncertainties.question: What Developer Site and tenant access can a workshop participant obtain without an existing Workday customer, partner, or Extend entitlement?
uncertainties.impact: Many documented paths cannot start without administrator invitation and provisioned tenant context.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official public documentation of any fully self-serve developer program, or confirmation that entitlement is always required.

uncertainties.question: What exact signup fields exist for Developer Site account creation when invitation is available?
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated invited-user signup walkthrough listing required fields.
